B
Control Grade
82.5/100

Controlled, minor gaps. Primarily capped by change traceability.

heuristic3 of 7 controls measured
Repository: sample-repo

Dimensions — 3 of 7 measurable

OK25%1.00

Requirement traceability

requirement-to-work traceability (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148)

1/1 FRs covered; 2/2 changes traced (FR-linked or classified no-FR)

route/feature inference + git-log classification (heuristic) · heuristic · @ deadbeefcafe
N/A20%N/A

Test health

automated tests pass (OpenSSF Scorecard)

3 test files across 1 framework (pytest) — present, not executed

static test inventory — present, not executed · unavailable
Would light: ci-junit-pass-ratio, scorecard-check-runs
GAP15%0.50

Change traceability

change provenance (SLSA)

1/2 changes linked to a commit, ADR or test run

git-log PR/issue links (heuristic) · heuristic · @ deadbeefcafe
N/A15%N/A

Change reconciliation

re-verify changed requirements (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207)

not measurable — needs per-change behavior-impact (BP-2)

Shipwright-only: per-change behaviour re-verification (BP-2) · unavailable
Would light: behavior-impact-ledger
N/A10%N/A

Security

no open high/critical vulns (NIST SSDF)

no code-scanning ingested (local-only)

deferred to G2: code-scanning SARIF / local scan · unavailable
Would light: code-scanning-sarif, local-scan
GAP10%0.85

Size / maintainability discipline

bounded module size (ISO/IEC 25010)

2/13 source files over 300 LOC

deferred to G2: oversize-file ratio · heuristic · @ deadbeefcafe
N/A5%N/A

Dependency hygiene

dependency license & risk (OWASP)

no dependency manifest

deferred to G2: lockfile → SBOM licenses · unavailable
Would light: lockfile-sbom

Controls Shipwright would light up

N/A on a cold repo — excluded from the score, never counted as a failed 0. These are the controls Shipwright would light up once adopted.

  • Test health
  • Change reconciliation
  • Security
  • Dependency hygiene

Top fixable reasons

  1. Change traceability: 1/2 changes linked to a commit, ADR or test run
Honest ceiling. Heuristic estimate from the outside — it inspects history and structure, it does not verify behaviour. A cold-repo grade cannot certify correctness.

Raise your grade — adopt Shipwright

The controls greyed out as N/A above aren't things you're doing wrong — they're controls a repo can't prove from the outside: a CI test signal, a security scan, and a change log that ties each change back to a requirement. Shipwright adds exactly that to your existing repo — no rewrite.

  • Reads first, doesn't touch. /shipwright-adopt analyses your stack, routes, conventions and git history and proposes the setup as a reviewable PR — it never rewrites your code.
  • Scaffolds the missing controls. It generates the traceability, CI, security-scan and dependency setup the N/A dimensions measure — so they become measured, not unknown from outside.
  • Every future change stays controlled. From then on /shipwright-iterate runs each change through tests, review and a security gate, and records it in a traceable event log.

Your next step: open your repo in Claude Code and run /shipwright-adopt. It's incremental and reversible.

New to Shipwright? Learn how it works → svenroth.ai/shipwright
Test inventory:3 test files across 1 framework (pytest) — present, not executed; CI workflow present
Network:local-only (default) — pass --allow-network to enrich via GitHub
verified_from:shipwright-grade heuristic @ deadbeefcafe1234 (local-only)
